source.section: 2 - Air-to-Ground Weapons > 2.11 - AGM-84E SLAM Cruise Missile > 2.11.2 - Weapon Setup

steps.voiced: Set Master Arm switch to ARM.

steps.action: On the SMS (Stores) page, press OSB next to SLAM to power up the AGM-84E missile.
steps.voiced: On the SMS page, press the OSB next to SLAM to power up the missile.

steps.action: On the SMS (Stores) page, click on the OSB next to DL13 to turn on the Datalink Pod.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to DL13 to turn on the Datalink Pod.

steps.action: Use STEP function to select desired SLAM missile station.
steps.voiced: Use STEP to select the desired SLAM missile station.

steps.action: Once selected, the SLAM needs about 3 minutes for alignment.
steps.voiced: Allow approximately 3 minutes for SLAM alignment.

steps.action: Alignment It will go from 10 UNSTABLE, to 06 MARGINAL and finally 01 GOOD. When alignment is complete, the TIMING indicator will disappear from the SMS page.
steps.voiced: Confirm alignment progresses UNSTABLE to MARGINAL to GOOD — TIMING disappears when complete.

steps.action: Press OSB (Option Select Button) next to WEP. This will allow you to choose what SLAM missile is communicating with the Datalink pod.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to WEP to pair SLAM with the Datalink pod.

steps.action: Select corresponding SLAM station using the OSBs on the left side. In our case, the lower SLAM (yellow) OSB needs to be slaved to Station 8 (boxed).
steps.voiced: Select the corresponding SLAM station OSB to slave it to the Datalink pod.

steps.action: Once communication between SLAM station and datalink pod has been established, a SLAM indication will be displayed under the boxed DL13 indication.
steps.voiced: Confirm SLAM indication appears under the boxed DL13 indication.

steps.action: Press OSB next to FLT (Fly-Out Altitude) parameter to toggle between HIGH (35,000 ft), MED (15,000 ft) and LOW (5,000 ft). We will set it to MED.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to FLT to set fly-out altitude — HIGH, MED, or LOW.

steps.action: Press OSB next to EFUZ (Electronic Fuze) to toggle parameter to INST (Instantaneous).
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to EFUZ and set it to INST.

steps.action: Press OSB next to SLAM DSPLY to enter the SLAM DISPLAY page.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to SLAM DSPLY to enter the SLAM Display page.

steps.action: Once you are in the SLAM DISPLAY page, press OSB next to REL TYPE (Release Type), then press OSB next to MAN to select a Manual Release.
steps.voiced: Press OSB next to REL TYPE, then press MAN to select Manual Release.

steps.action: Press OSB next to UFC (Up-Front Control). Once UFC is boxed, the DIST parameter (distance from target when seeker head goes active) can be modified from the UFC panel.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to UFC to box it — DIST parameter is now editable.

steps.action: Press OSB next to DIST to select this field. ":" indicates the field is selected. Then, enter "15" on the UFC keypad, then press "ENT" button to set a DIST parameter of 15 nm.
steps.voiced: Press OSB next to DIST, enter 15 on the UFC keypad, then press ENT.

steps.action: Press OSB next to UFC to unselect (unbox) the function.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to UFC again to unbox it.
